L1.area: Serialization protocol configuration and runtime class resolution

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the protection mechanism intended to restrict deserialization of dangerous classes can be bypassed using a gadget chain composed entirely of JDK classes, without relying on any third-party components. This allows an attacker to achieve remote command execution by crafting malicious serialized data.

L3.area: Deserialization class restriction configuration and its internal filtering logic
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the blacklist-based protection mechanism used to restrict deserialization of dangerous classes in the Hessian protocol can be bypassed. A gadget chain composed entirely of JDK classes, without relying on any third-party components, can circumvent the existing class restrictions and achieve remote command execution.

ground_truth.reason: The deserialization blacklist was insufficient and could be bypassed by a JDK-only gadget chain, enabling remote command execution through crafted Hessian protocol messages.
